response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist software developer with strong web, full-stack, and some systems-level background (DLang low-latency services, C/C++ at National Instruments), but they lack nearly all core requirements for this role: no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no embedded LAN-switch or firmware work, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 networking stacks. The profile is a fundamental mismatch despite general seniority.
strengths: 13+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments and low-latency asynchronous DLang services
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency indicated — a hard requirement (native level)
No Linux kernel analysis/modification, embedded firmware, or LAN-switch product experience
No demonstrated experience with required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC auth) or L2/L3 networking protocols

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Solid general seniority and some C/C++ systems background
